headline: Zoonotic Disease Surveillance Covers Only 35% of Wildlife Markets
context: Disease surveillance at live animal markets and wildlife trade points remains critically inadequate post-COVID.

cards.big: Only 35% of 420 identified wildlife trade points have any disease surveillance in 2025
cards.sub: Post-COVID lessons about zoonotic spillover have not translated to funded monitoring

cards.big: Malaysia borders the highest-density bat coronavirus reservoir in Southeast Asia
cards.sub: Deforestation drives wildlife into human contact zones increasing spillover risk

cards.big: DVS veterinary surveillance budget was cut 15% in 2025 from already inadequate levels
cards.sub: Animal health monitoring directly prevents human pandemic threats

cards.big: WHO One Health approach requires integrated human-animal-environment disease monitoring
cards.sub: Siloed health and veterinary systems miss cross-species disease emergence signals

cards.big: The next pandemic will likely start where a wildlife market meets a surveillance gap
Should Malaysia mandate comprehensive disease surveillance at all wildlife trade points
